preamble: The shift of a high back nasalized vowel [ũ] to a diphthong [au] involves diphthongization, often as part of a larger chain shift or as a result of vowel lowering in nasal environments.

examples.note: Historical stage in the development of Latin high back vowels towards modern French nasal vowels, involving a stage of diphthongization and lowering.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
